tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All F2P tests (test_open_context_modal_signature, test_context_has_close_all, test_update_context_modal_no_modal_key, etc.) use `read_file()` and regex/string matching on source code text. None import or call the actual code. The P2P tests (jest, eslint, prettier, tsc) do execute subprocesses, but every F2P test is purely text-matching with `assert 'X' in open(path).read()` style checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: The F2P tests check for specific patterns that are NOT present in the buggy base commit (e.g., two-argument openContextModal signature, closeAll instead of closeAllModals, absence of ContextModalInnerProps). A stub/no-op would not produce these patterns in the source files, so actual code changes are required.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/mantine (the solve.sh writes files there). The agent could read the test file at tests/test_outputs.py to see exact regex patterns expected, or read /solution/solve.sh if accessible. Since the tests are pure text-matching on source files, the agent could simply grep the test file for the expected patterns and write them verbatim without understanding the bug.
